# Viewer IFC · BCF

> Free and open-source IFC and BCF viewer that runs entirely in the browser. Open and federate IFC models, review BCF issues and Solibri reports, inspect elements and their property sets, slice with a section box, edit properties in the 3D view and re-export an updated IFC. 100% client-side: models never leave the user's computer — no upload, no account, no server.

Key facts:
- License: GNU AGPL-3.0 (open source).
- Privacy by design: IFC parsing happens locally in the browser via web-ifc (WebAssembly). No file is uploaded; no account or tracking.
- Single-file app: plain HTML/CSS/JS, no framework, no build step. Dependencies (three.js, web-ifc) are loaded from a CDN on first launch.
- Supported formats: IFC2x3, IFC4, IFC4x3, BCF, Solibri report.
- It is a coordination viewer, not just a visualization viewer: federation, BCF issue review, property editing (including bulk) and IFC re-export.
- Author: Alessandro Perugini (BIM Coordinator).
- Bilingual interface: Italian and English.

## Pages

- [Landing page](https://viewifc.com/): overview, features, privacy model and links.
- [Viewer (the app)](https://viewifc.com/viewer/): the in-browser IFC/BCF viewer itself.
- [Guide](https://viewifc.com/guide/): quick-start, how to load models, navigate, open BCF, work on issues and edit parameters.

## Tools

Companion tools that run entirely in the browser (no upload, no install), in the same client-side, privacy-by-design spirit as the viewer.

- [IFC Class Patcher](https://viewifc.com/tools/ifc-class-patcher/): bulk-reclassifies IFC elements from an Excel mapping (Class → IFC entity → ENUMTYPE). For each element matched by a configurable reference property (auto-suggested from the loaded IFC) it rewrites both the IFC class (the STEP entity keyword, e.g. IFCBUILDINGELEMENTPROXY → IFCKERB) and the PredefinedType. The user loads an IFC file and an Excel mapping, picks which columns to use, and downloads a corrected IFC. Bilingual (Italian/English).
- [IFC Progressiva Converter](https://viewifc.com/tools/ifc-progressiva-converter/): converts road chainage (progressiva) values stored on IFC elements between plain metres and the kilometric format K+MMM,CC (e.g. 1+234,56), in both directions — metres → text (IFCTEXT) and back to a number (IFCREAL). The user picks which properties to convert (auto-suggested from the loaded IFC). Bilingual (Italian/English).
- [IFC Alignment to LandXML](https://viewifc.com/tools/ifc-alignment-to-landxml/): extracts the IfcAlignment entities from an IFC4x3 file and exports them as LandXML 1.2 — horizontal geometry (Line/Curve/Spiral clothoid) and vertical profile (PVI/ParaCurve) — ready to load into Dalux. Reads the IFC structure with a lightweight client-side STEP parser; the EPSG code and the coordinate/azimuth conventions are configurable. The user loads an IFC file and downloads a LandXML (.xml). Bilingual (Italian/English).

## Source & license

- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/arsray146/ifc-bcf-viewer): full source code (AGPL-3.0).
- [AGPL-3.0 license](https://www.gnu.org/licenses/agpl-3.0.html): terms of use and redistribution.

## Tech stack

- Rendering: three.js 0.160.0 (MIT).
- IFC parsing: web-ifc 0.0.77 (MPL-2.0), WebAssembly.
- Fonts: Barlow Semi Condensed, IBM Plex Sans, IBM Plex Mono.

## Contact

- Email: info@viewifc.com

- [Sitemap](https://viewifc.com/sitemap.xml): machine-readable list of public URLs.
